findings.title: SIMULTANEOUS CID CO-PRESENCE -- BAND INCOMPATIBILITY PROVEN [CRITICAL]
findings.description: Analysis of 190 co-presence windows where multiple rogue CIDs appear simultaneously. Physically incompatible band combinations detected -- single-chain SDR operation ruled out. Two transmitters required.
findings.technique: Co-presence window analysis (60s window); RSRP offset consistency measurement; band incompatibility analysis per 3GPP TS 36.104; RF chain frequency coverage analysis
evidence: SIMULTANEOUS MULTI-BAND OPERATION CONFIRMED: 166 instances of physically incompatible band pairs observed simultaneously. Single-chain SDR operation is RULED OUT. Two devices or multi-chain professional hardware required. Combined with srsRAN timing signature: TWO DEVICES CONFIRMED.
CO-PRESENCE ANALYSIS: 190 windows found where multiple rogue CIDs observed within 60s of each other.

BAND CO-PRESENCE PROOF: Band 7 and Band 28 observed simultaneously in 113 windows. Frequency ratio: 4.00x. Reason incompatible: 700MHz vs 2600MHz -- 3.71x frequency ratio. A SINGLE SDR RF CHAIN CANNOT SIMULTANEOUSLY TRANSMIT ON BOTH BANDS. This observation is PHYSICALLY IMPOSSIBLE from a single-chain consumer SDR device. It requires EITHER: (a) Professional multi-chain hardware (Harris/Septier), OR (b) Two separate devices on different bands. Combined with the srsRAN timing signature (2.10s intervals), which professional hardware cannot produce, the only consistent explanation is TWO DEVICES: one professional (multi-band), one consumer SDR (srsRAN).

BAND CO-PRESENCE PROOF: Band 1 and Band 28 observed simultaneously in 26 windows. Frequency ratio: 28.00x. Reason incompatible: 700MHz vs 2100MHz -- 3.0x frequency ratio. A SINGLE SDR RF CHAIN CANNOT SIMULTANEOUSLY TRANSMIT ON BOTH BANDS. This observation is PHYSICALLY IMPOSSIBLE from a single-chain consumer SDR device. It requires EITHER: (a) Professional multi-chain hardware (Harris/Septier), OR (b) Two separate devices on different bands. Combined with the srsRAN timing signature (2.10s intervals), which professional hardware cannot produce, the only consistent explanation is TWO DEVICES: one professional (multi-band), one consumer SDR (srsRAN).

BAND CO-PRESENCE PROOF: Band 3 and Band 28 observed simultaneously in 27 windows. Frequency ratio: 9.33x. Reason incompatible: 700MHz vs 1800MHz -- 2.43x frequency ratio. A SINGLE SDR RF CHAIN CANNOT SIMULTANEOUSLY TRANSMIT ON BOTH BANDS. This observation is PHYSICALLY IMPOSSIBLE from a single-chain consumer SDR device. It requires EITHER: (a) Professional multi-chain hardware (Harris/Septier), OR (b) Two separate devices on different bands. Combined with the srsRAN timing signature (2.10s intervals), which professional hardware cannot produce, the only consistent explanation is TWO DEVICES: one professional (multi-band), one consumer SDR (srsRAN).

FORENSIC SIGNIFICANCE: Band co-presence data is derived from CASTNET observations -- timestamped, logged, and independently reproducible. Each band co-presence event has a precise AEST timestamp. This evidence exists independently of any device audit or corporate compliance process. It cannot be explained away by a clean audit of single-band equipment.

heuristics.evidence: Zero forced-2G-downgrade events detected. Sumailov (2023, Univ. Tartu) confirmed experimentally: modern smartphones prefer 4G over rogue 2G BTS regardless of signal strength. Transparent proxy is the only silent LTE interception method without jamming artefacts. NOTE: EEA0 null-cipher on LTE (heuristic 4.1.6) is a separate indicator — it does not mean 2G downgrade.

Profile: non-transparent-proxy (srsRAN / PKI / cheap commercial class).
